text.name: system_description
text.text: FMV games use pre-recorded video. The term emerged in the late 1980s when home computers became powerful enough for live-action video, and CD-ROMs in the early 1990s allowed full video playback. FMV titles developed from simple cutscenes to interactive movies and fully video-driven games. Before CDs and DVDs there were interactive VHS games playable on a VCR. As technology got better, players could shoot at the television screen with a light gun while an attached console would keep score.
